cases.one_liner: Consumer symptom education app that explains common symptoms and suggests when to consult a doctor.
cases.intended_use: Provide general health education and care-seeking guidance without diagnosis or treatment advice.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely non-device if limited to education and no diagnosis or treatment recommendations are made
cases.rationale: General symptom education without diagnosis or treatment recommendation is likely non-device. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk health information software.

cases.one_liner: Remote patient monitoring dashboard that displays vitals from connected home devices.
cases.intended_use: Allow clinicians to view patient vitals trends and receive threshold-based alerts.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class B if used for patient monitoring; lower risk if passive display only
cases.rationale: Real-time vitals display with threshold-based alerts for clinician review is active patient monitoring, so Class B is the safer label. Class A would only fit passive fitness-style display without clinical alerts.

cases.one_liner: AI system that screens retinal fundus images for diabetic retinopathy risk.
cases.intended_use: Assist screening programs by flagging patients who need ophthalmologist referral.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD requiring clinical validation
cases.rationale: AI diabetic retinopathy screening influences diagnosis/referral for a serious condition. Class B may apply only if strictly preliminary triage with mandatory specialist review.

cases.one_liner: AI model that detects pneumonia patterns from chest X-rays.
cases.intended_use: Assist clinicians and radiologists in identifying possible pneumonia from chest radiographs.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD; risk increases if used for emergency or autonomous diagnosis
cases.rationale: AI chest X-ray pneumonia detection can drive clinical diagnosis and treatment for serious disease. Class D may apply if used for emergency or autonomous diagnosis.

cases.one_liner: Hospital billing, inventory, and administrative workflow management platform.
cases.intended_use: Digitize non-clinical hospital operations such as billing, stock, and administrative approvals.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device because it does not diagnose, treat, or monitor patients
cases.rationale: Hospital billing, inventory, and administrative workflow software is not intended for medical diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring. It should remain outside CDSCO device classification.

cases.one_liner: Medication adherence app that reminds patients to take prescribed medicines and alerts caregivers.
cases.intended_use: Improve adherence to prescribed medication without changing dosage or treatment.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Usually low-risk patient management software; regulated risk rises if dosage/treatment is changed
cases.rationale: Reminder-only medication adherence app without active dose recommendation is wellness/non-device. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk health information software with any borderline clinical positioning.
cases.label_correction_reason: Bucket B from Story 1.3 recon. Original label was too aggressive for reminder-only adherence.

cases.one_liner: AI system that analyzes ECG signals and flags possible arrhythmias.
cases.intended_use: Assist clinicians in detecting possible arrhythmias from ECG data.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD because output supports diagnosis of cardiovascular conditions
cases.rationale: AI ECG arrhythmia detection supports diagnosis of potentially serious cardiovascular conditions. Class B may apply if it only flags recordings for clinician review without diagnostic claims.

cases.one_liner: Closed-loop software that recommends or controls insulin delivery based on CGM readings.
cases.intended_use: Recommend or automate insulin dosing adjustments for diabetic patients.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class C SaMD for standalone dosing recommendation; may inherit Class D risk if integrated with an autonomous insulin pump
cases.rationale: Standalone insulin dose recommendation is high-risk treatment decision software and should be treated at least as Class C with Class D-level safety controls. Class D may apply if integrated with or controlling a high-risk insulin pump/autonomous closed loop.

cases.one_liner: Digital physiotherapy app that guides prescribed exercises and tracks adherence.
cases.intended_use: Support patients in completing physiotherapy exercises prescribed by clinicians.
cases.regulatory_pathway: May be Class B if used for rehabilitation management; lower if education-only
applicable_frameworks: CDSCO MDR if therapeutic claims are made
cases.rationale: Digital physiotherapy guidance supports rehabilitation management. Class A may apply where it only provides exercise education and adherence tracking.

cases.one_liner: Hospital AI that predicts sepsis risk using vitals, labs, and EHR data.
cases.intended_use: Alert clinicians about patients at elevated risk of sepsis.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely high-risk SaMD because it can drive urgent intervention
cases.rationale: Sepsis prediction influences urgent clinical management for a critical condition. Class D may apply if alerts directly trigger emergency intervention without clinician confirmation.

cases.one_liner: Consumer nutrition and fitness coaching app with calorie tracking and activity plans.
cases.intended_use: Help users track diet, activity, and wellness goals.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely wellness-only and outside CDSCO device scope unless disease-specific claims are introduced
cases.rationale: Consumer nutrition and fitness coaching is likely wellness-only. Class A may apply if explicit health-condition monitoring claims are introduced.

cases.one_liner: Portable spirometry app that captures lung function readings for remote review.
cases.intended_use: Measure and monitor lung function for patients with respiratory conditions.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated medical device/software linked to diagnostic monitoring
cases.rationale: Remote spirometry supports clinical monitoring of respiratory disease. Class C may apply if diagnostic or treatment escalation claims are made.

cases.one_liner: Mobile imaging AI that flags suspicious oral lesions for referral.
cases.intended_use: Support frontline workers in identifying possible oral cancer lesions needing specialist referral.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD requiring clinical evidence
cases.rationale: AI oral cancer screening supports detection/referral for a serious condition. Class B may apply only for very limited triage with clear clinician confirmation.

cases.one_liner: ABDM consent manager that enables consent-based health record exchange.
cases.intended_use: Enable consent-based exchange of digital health records using ABDM rails.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device if limited to consent and data exchange
cases.rationale: ABDM consent manager or health record exchange software is health IT infrastructure, not a medical device. No CDSCO device class should be assigned.

cases.one_liner: Smartphone app that tracks wound healing progress and sends updates to clinicians.
cases.intended_use: Track wound size and healing status for clinician review.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class B clinical monitoring software; Class C if it diagnoses infection or recommends treatment
cases.rationale: Wound monitoring software supports clinical monitoring and follow-up. Class C may apply if it diagnoses infection or recommends treatment changes.

cases.one_liner: Mobile app that logs glucose readings and gives lifestyle nudges for diabetics.
cases.intended_use: Support self-management of diabetes through reminders and trend insights.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class A if positioned as diabetes monitoring; non-device only if wellness-only logging
applicable_frameworks: ABDM if health record exchange is enabled
cases.rationale: Glucose trend visualization for diabetes self-management is low-risk chronic disease monitoring software. It may be non-device only if claims are restricted to wellness logging without diabetes management language.

cases.one_liner: AI model that reads ECG images and flags possible myocardial infarction.
cases.intended_use: Assist clinicians in identifying high-risk ECGs for urgent review.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Medical device software, likely CDSCO regulated
cases.rationale: ECG myocardial infarction triage drives clinical management for a serious or critical condition. Class D may apply if used for emergency diagnosis without adequate clinician review.

cases.one_liner: Smartphone-based skin lesion classifier for suspicious mole detection.
cases.intended_use: Flag suspicious skin lesions and recommend dermatologist consultation.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely SaMD requiring clinical validation
cases.rationale: Skin cancer risk flagging influences diagnosis and referral for a serious condition. This fits Class C SaMD.

cases.one_liner: XR-based surgical planning tool for orthopedic implant placement.
cases.intended_use: Assist surgeons with pre-operative visualization and planning.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely medical device software depending on clinical claims
cases.rationale: Surgical planning software influences clinical management but remains clinician-supervised. Class C may apply if it directly determines implant placement or surgical action.

cases.one_liner: Home fetal heartbeat listening device connected to a mobile app.
cases.intended_use: Allow pregnant women to listen to fetal heartbeat at home.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely CDSCO regulated medical device
cases.rationale: Home fetal heartbeat monitoring can influence pregnancy care-seeking. Class C may apply if diagnostic fetal distress claims are made.

cases.one_liner: AI chatbot for stress journaling and mental wellness support.
cases.intended_use: Offer guided journaling and non-clinical emotional support.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Wellness product if no diagnosis, therapy, or crisis management claims
cases.rationale: Non-clinical mental wellness journaling is likely outside medical device scope. If it claims mental health screening or clinical management, Class A may be the lower-bound class.

cases.one_liner: AI model that analyzes CT brain scans to detect possible stroke.
cases.intended_use: Assist radiologists in prioritizing suspected stroke cases.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD with high clinical risk
cases.rationale: Stroke CT triage relates to a critical condition and can drive urgent clinical management. Class C may apply if strictly prioritization-only with radiologist confirmation.

cases.one_liner: Tool that helps clinics create ABHA-linked digital health records.
cases.intended_use: Digitize and exchange patient records using ABDM rails.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device unless clinical decision claims are added
cases.rationale: ABDM record creation and exchange is health IT infrastructure. It does not diagnose, treat, or monitor a disease.

cases.one_liner: AI tool that suggests likely cancer grading from pathology slide images.
cases.intended_use: Assist pathologists in reviewing biopsy slides.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated high-risk SaMD
cases.rationale: Cancer grading support influences diagnosis and clinical management for a serious disease. Class D may apply if output is used as primary diagnosis.

cases.one_liner: Computer vision app that tracks physiotherapy exercise adherence.
cases.intended_use: Help patients perform prescribed rehab exercises correctly.
cases.regulatory_pathway: May be medical device software if used for clinical rehabilitation decisions
applicable_frameworks: CDSCO MDR if therapeutic claims are made
cases.rationale: Physiotherapy tracking supports rehabilitation management but is not generally life-critical. Class A may apply if only adherence or wellness feedback is claimed.

cases.one_liner: Predictive model that alerts ICU teams about patient deterioration risk.
cases.intended_use: Predict deterioration risk using vitals and lab parameters.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely high-risk clinical decision support SaMD
cases.rationale: ICU deterioration prediction drives clinical management in a critical care environment. Class D may apply if the alert directly triggers emergency intervention.

cases.one_liner: AI assistant that helps hospitals prepare insurance pre-authorization documents.
cases.intended_use: Reduce administrative burden in claims documentation.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device if no clinical decision-making is involved
cases.rationale: Insurance pre-authorization documentation is administrative and does not influence clinical diagnosis or treatment. It should not be classified as a medical device.

cases.one_liner: Software that calculates pediatric drug dosage based on age, weight, and diagnosis.
cases.intended_use: Support clinicians with dosage calculations.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated clinical decision support software
cases.rationale: Pediatric drug dose calculation affects medication safety and clinical treatment. Class B may apply only for transparent calculator-style support with full clinician verification.

cases.one_liner: Wearable ring that tracks sleep quality, HRV, and recovery.
cases.intended_use: Track wellness and recovery patterns.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely wellness device if no diagnosis or treatment claims
cases.rationale: Consumer sleep, HRV, and recovery tracking is wellness-only if no diagnosis or treatment claims are made. Class A may apply if medical monitoring claims are introduced.

cases.one_liner: AI tool that screens chest X-rays for signs of pulmonary tuberculosis.
cases.intended_use: Assist TB screening programs by flagging suspicious X-rays.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated SaMD
cases.rationale: TB chest X-ray screening supports detection of a serious infectious disease. Class B may apply if used only for workflow prioritization and not diagnostic output.

cases.one_liner: Dashboard that predicts dengue outbreak risk using weather and case data.
cases.intended_use: Support public health teams with early warning and hotspot prioritization.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely not a patient-facing medical device; public health decision-support governance needed
cases.rationale: Dengue hotspot forecasting is population-level public health planning, not patient-specific diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring. It should remain outside CDSCO medical device classification.

cases.one_liner: LLM assistant for nurses to summarize patient notes and suggest care checklist items.
cases.intended_use: Assist nurses in documentation and care coordination.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Depends on whether checklist suggestions influence clinical care
cases.rationale: Nursing checklist generation can influence clinical workflow and patient management. Class A may apply if documentation-only with mandatory human review.

cases.one_liner: Mobile imaging tool that flags possible oral cancer lesions.
cases.intended_use: Support frontline screening for suspicious oral lesions.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated AI SaMD requiring clinical evidence
cases.rationale: Oral cancer lesion flagging supports screening and referral for a serious condition. This fits Class C SaMD.

cases.one_liner: AI tool that predicts OPD crowding and suggests appointment slots.
cases.intended_use: Improve patient flow and reduce waiting times.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device
cases.rationale: OPD queue optimization is hospital operations software and does not diagnose, treat, or monitor patients. It should remain outside CDSCO medical device scope.

cases.one_liner: Wearable patch that continuously estimates blood pressure.
cases.intended_use: Continuously monitor blood pressure trends.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated medical device
cases.rationale: Continuous blood pressure estimation supports monitoring and management of hypertension. Class B may apply if only trend display is claimed without clinical decision support.

cases.one_liner: AI tool that predicts high-risk pregnancy from ANC records.
cases.intended_use: Help health workers identify pregnant women needing closer follow-up.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely clinical decision support SaMD if used for care prioritization
cases.rationale: High-risk pregnancy prediction influences care prioritization for maternal health. Class B may apply if used only for non-diagnostic referral support.

cases.one_liner: Consumer app that explains lab reports in simple language.
cases.intended_use: Help users understand lab values and ask better questions to doctors.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class A if interpreting lab values; non-device only if generic education without health-status interpretation
cases.rationale: Lab report explanation that interprets clinical values into health status crosses from pure education into low-risk interpretive medical software. It may be non-device only if it strictly avoids interpretation and provides generic educational content.

cases.one_liner: App that recommends insulin dose changes based on CGM and meal logs.
cases.intended_use: Recommend insulin adjustments for diabetic patients.
cases.regulatory_pathway: High-risk regulated SaMD
cases.rationale: Insulin dose recommendation directly drives therapy for a potentially life-threatening condition. This should be treated as Class D SaMD.

cases.one_liner: WhatsApp bot that books appointments and collects patient intake details.
cases.intended_use: Automate appointment booking and intake forms.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device
cases.rationale: Appointment booking and intake automation is administrative. It is not intended for medical diagnosis, treatment, or monitoring.

cases.one_liner: Connected nebulizer that tracks medication sessions and adherence.
cases.intended_use: Support respiratory patients and care teams by tracking nebulization sessions and adherence, without automatically adjusting therapy.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated device if linked to treatment delivery
cases.rationale: A smart nebulizer app that tracks treatment adherence is Class B patient/treatment support. Class C applies if the software adjusts nebulization sessions, controls therapy, or changes treatment instructions.

cases.one_liner: Software that generates orthodontic aligner plans from dental scans.
cases.intended_use: Assist orthodontists in planning aligner treatment.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely medical device software
cases.rationale: Orthodontic aligner treatment planning influences therapeutic intervention. Class B may apply only if it is simulation-only and not used to finalize treatment plans.

cases.one_liner: Wearable fall detection device for elderly users.
cases.intended_use: Detect falls and alert caregivers.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely Class A safety monitoring device/software if no diagnostic or neurological claims are made
cases.rationale: Fall detection and fall-prevention alarms are low-risk safety monitoring when they do not make neurological or diagnostic claims. Class B may apply only if positioned as clinical patient monitoring for a disease condition.

cases.one_liner: Clinical tool that recommends antibiotic choices based on symptoms and local resistance data.
cases.intended_use: Support clinicians in selecting antibiotics.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely regulated clinical decision support SaMD
cases.rationale: Antibiotic recommendation affects prescribing and treatment for potentially serious infections. Class D may apply where incorrect output could cause critical harm or antimicrobial failure.

cases.one_liner: Platform that matches patients to clinical trials using diagnosis and location.
cases.intended_use: Help patients discover potentially relevant clinical trials.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Usually not medical device if informational and not treatment recommending
cases.rationale: Clinical trial matching is informational if it does not recommend treatment. Class A may apply if eligibility pre-screening is positioned as medical decision support.

cases.one_liner: Portable spirometer with app-based COPD monitoring.
cases.intended_use: Measure lung function remotely and support COPD monitoring.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely CDSCO regulated medical device
cases.rationale: Remote spirometry measures and monitors lung function. Class C may apply if diagnostic or treatment escalation claims are made.

cases.one_liner: AI medical scribe that converts doctor-patient conversations into structured notes.
cases.intended_use: Reduce clinician documentation burden.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Not a medical device if documentation-only; risk increases if diagnosis suggestions are added
cases.rationale: AI medical scribing is documentation support if no clinical recommendations are generated. Class A may apply if summaries influence low-risk clinical management.

cases.one_liner: AI app that tracks wound healing progress from smartphone photos.
cases.intended_use: Track wound size and healing progression for clinician review.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely SaMD if used for clinical monitoring
cases.rationale: Wound healing image tracking supports clinical monitoring. Class C may apply if it diagnoses infection, necrosis, or recommends treatment changes.

cases.one_liner: App that predicts fertility window using cycle history and symptoms.
cases.intended_use: Help users track cycle and estimate fertile window.
cases.regulatory_pathway: May be wellness unless contraceptive or infertility treatment claims are made
cases.rationale: Cycle and fertility tracking is usually wellness if not contraceptive or infertility treatment software. Class B may apply if used for contraception or fertility treatment decisions.

cases.one_liner: Hospital AI system that predicts sepsis risk from EHR and lab data.
cases.intended_use: Alert clinicians about patients at risk of sepsis.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Likely high-risk SaMD
cases.rationale: Sepsis prediction relates to a critical condition and can drive urgent intervention. Class C may apply only with strict clinician confirmation and prioritization-only claims.

cases.one_liner: App that reminds patients to take medicines and rewards adherence.
cases.intended_use: Improve medication adherence through reminders and incentives.
cases.regulatory_pathway: Usually not medical device unless dosing or treatment decisions are included
cases.rationale: Medication reminders and rewards are adherence support, not treatment decision software. Class A may apply if positioned as low-risk patient management software.

change_summary: Updated CP-002 to Class B because threshold-based clinician alerts are active monitoring.
Updated CP-042 to Class A with Class B acceptable only for disease monitoring/neurological claims.
Updated CP-008 to Class C with Class D acceptable when integrated with autonomous/high-risk pump control.
Updated CP-037 and CP-016 to Class A due to interpretive lab/glucose monitoring claims.
Kept CP-031 non-device/null with population-level public health rationale.
Added SBOM and cybersecurity risk assessment to Class B/C/D cases.
Added DPIA/consent-manager readiness docs to sensitive health-data and AI cases where DPDP applies.
Added LLM hallucination mitigation documentation to CP-032.
